commit.message:
Add audio sharing to watches, fix 16 prompt glitches across Night & Morning Watch

Audio Sharing (ImmersiveAudioPlayer):
- Share Audio button appears in top bar when audio URL is available
- Copy Audio Link: copies direct MP3 URL for pasting in text messages
- Send Audio File: uses native share API to send MP3 via iMessage/WhatsApp
- Download MP3: saves audio file directly to device

Prompt Glitch Fixes (both Night & Morning Watch):
- Removed verbatim phrases that appeared in BOTH sample AND instructions,
  causing the AI to repeat them (e.g. "Download this into me", "He sees you.
  Not the crowd. You.", "You can feel it — quiet, steady", "Not striving.
  Not performing.", chest tightness pattern)
- Changed "Match this rhythm exactly" to "Study the QUALITY and FEEL but
  do NOT copy specific phrases" — resolves contradiction with variety rules
- Added alternative neuroplasticity anchors to Night Watch (was only in
  Morning Watch before, causing Night to repeat same phrase nightly)
- Added alternative "receiving Christ's mind" declarations to Night Watch
- Removed "Say it quietly: I receive the mind of Christ" as hardcoded
  anchor — now varied each session
- Cleaned up instruction examples that echoed sample text verbatim

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(compatibility): BEE-9 add cross-repo matrix + update script + GH Action (#3)

## 📝 Summary

Closes [BEE-9](https://linear.app/beeping/issue/BEE-9) — 📊 Compatibility
matrix cross-repo + automatización.

Builds the cross-repo compatibility tracking infrastructure for the
Beeping Platform: a matrix file with auto-generated and hand-written
sections, a JSON Schema validating its shape, a bash script that
regenerates the matrix from the GitHub API, and a weekly GitHub Action
that runs the script and opens a PR when drift is detected.

## 🎯 Type of change

- [x] ✨ `feat` — new feature (compatibility matrix infrastructure)
- [x] 🤖 `ci` — new GitHub Action workflow

## 📋 Changes

### `compatibility.md` (rewritten)

- Auto-generated section between `<!-- BEGIN AUTO-GENERATED -->` / `<!--
END AUTO-GENERATED -->` markers
- Hand-written sections for rationale, OpenAPI versions, guarantees,
cross-repo contracts, breaking change protocol
- Initial table with all 18 repos at placeholder version `0.0.0`

### `compatibility-schema.json` (new)

- JSON Schema (draft 2020-12) for the matrix structure
- Defines `openapi_versions[]` and `repos[]` shapes
- Reserved for future structural validation in CI

### `scripts/update-compatibility.sh` (new)

- Bash script that fetches `VERSION` from each of the 18 repos via
GitHub API
- Falls back to `0.0.0` if `VERSION` doesn't exist or repo is
inaccessible
- Uses Python (not awk) for the section replacement to avoid BSD awk
multiline string issues
- **Idempotent**: ignores the `Last refreshed` timestamp line when
comparing, so no-op runs don't bump the file
- Tested locally: generates correct table, second run is no-op

### `.github/workflows/update-compatibility.yml` (new)

- Weekly cron (Mondays 04:00 UTC) + manual `workflow_dispatch`
- Runs the script with the built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- Validates markers are present
- Opens a PR via `peter-evans/create-pull-request@v7` if changes
detected
- PR includes a review checklist (downgrades, contracts, ADR, lint,
smoke)
- Permissions scoped to `contents:write` + `pull-requests:write`

## ✅ Definition of Done

- [x] Smoke test passes (50/50 local)
- [x] Markdown lint clean
- [x] Gitleaks clean
- [x] JSON schema valid
- [x] Script tested locally — generates correct table, idempotent on
rerun
- [x] PR linked to a Linear task
- [x] Conventional Commits format used

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

3D liberation phases 6-10: ejection, recycling, passive advection, tidal

Completes the 3D physics liberation. Every force, damping, and advection
function now uses the local orbital frame (r_hat, t_hat, L_hat) instead
of hardcoded XZ-plane geometry.

Phase 6: Ejection kick uses t_hat (toroidal) and L_hat (poloidal) from
         local frame. Jets escape along the particle's actual orbital
         normal, not just along Y.

Phase 7: Boundary recycling respawns along 3D radial direction,
         preserving orbital plane orientation instead of snapping to
         XZ circle.

Phase 8: Passive advection uses Rodrigues rotation around L_hat instead
         of XZ-circle phi advance. Shell drift uses r3d. Velocity
         fix-up writes v_kep along 3D t_hat.

Phase 8b: Active region mask velocity fix-up computes local frame from
          current L on passive→active promotion.

Phase 10: Tidal shear uses r3d instead of r_cyl, removes directional
          L_disk_align penalty (uses |L_disk_align| for coherence).

Shells verified stable over 500 frames with flat-disk init conditions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

3D physics liberation: local orbital frame replaces hardcoded XZ plane

Phases 0-5 of the 3D liberation plan. Introduces compute_local_frame()
which derives radial/tangential/normal vectors from each particle's
angular momentum L = r × v. Particles can now orbit in ANY plane.

Changes:
- Phase 0: compute_local_frame() helper in forces.cuh
- Phase 1: Remove L_disk_align and height_penalty from coupling strength
- Phase 2: Coherence direction uses local frame (r_hat, t_hat, L_hat)
           instead of hardcoded (px,0,pz), (-pz,0,px), (0,1,0)
- Phase 3: Ion kick and core anchor inject energy in 3D radial direction
           (was XZ-only, no vy component)
- Phase 4: Keplerian restore uses 3D tangential/radial from local frame
- Phase 5: Disk damping replaced with orbital-plane damping (damps
           velocity perpendicular to L_hat, not just vy)

Shell positions verified stable over 5000 frames. No physics regression
with flat-disk initial conditions (local frames default to XZ).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(server): match fitness results to correct creatures via task_index

Workers return results in arbitrary order (fast creatures finish first),
but evaluate_generation assigned result[i] to task_map[i] — the i-th
result to the i-th task sent. This shuffled fitness across creatures,
destroying selection pressure and making evolution equivalent to random
search.

Fix: EvalTask/EvalResult now carry a task_index that workers echo back.
Results are matched by index, not arrival order.

Also: use NEEDS_EVAL sentinel (NEG_INFINITY) instead of 0.0 to mark
unevaluated creatures, preventing wasteful re-evaluation of creatures
that legitimately scored zero.

Fix post-login redirect: use hard navigation instead of SPA routing

causing the app to show the landing page after login. Use
window.location.href for a reliable full-page load with the session
cookie already set.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Port Adelia Risk header and footer to the EmDash site

Replaces the generic blog template chrome with brand-faithful Site
Header and Footer components modeled on adeliarisk.com:

- Header: white sticky bar, real Adelia Risk logo from R2, primary nav
  with Services and Resources dropdowns, search box, gradient phone
  CTA button, mobile hamburger toggle
- Footer: dark navy gradient background, four columns (brand+contact,
  Services, Company, Resources), copyright bar with theme switcher

Nav and footer link sets are scraped from the live adeliarisk.com
header/footer markup, deduped, and hardcoded in the components since
EmDash menus don't support nested submenus. Most outbound destinations
are stubbed to "/" on this throwaway site — only the migrated RIA
Cybersecurity Services page is wired up properly.

Base.astro is now a thin shell that imports SiteHeader/SiteFooter and
no longer fetches the seed menu or pages collection. The orphaned CSS
for the old .site-header/.site-footer/.theme-switcher classes is left
in place (dead but harmless) and can be cleaned up in a follow-up.

Built and deployed to Cloudflare Workers — live now at the production
URL. package-lock.json is intentionally not committed (project favors
pnpm per pnpm-workspace.yaml).

commit.message:
Redirect all output to stderr and add verbose flag

pkl-proxy was writing diagnostic output to stdout, which would
contaminate the output of commands run via `pkl-proxy run`. All
output now goes to stderr, and informational proxy messages (server
startup, installation discovery, request logging) are gated behind
a -v/--verbose flag.

commit.message:
refactor(server): in-memory engine with channel-based workers

Replace the DB-as-message-queue architecture with crossbeam channels
and an in-memory evolution engine. Workers receive tasks via channel
(not DB polling), coordinators keep current generation in memory
(not re-reading from DB), and API handlers serve from engine snapshots
(not DB queries). SQLite is relegated to write-behind archival.

Eliminates ~1070 DB ops/5s on the hot path (claim_task, complete_task,
load_gen, poll_pending, API reads) → ~2 writes/generation.

Also defaults num_signal_channels to 0 (paper-faithful).

§17-18: post_reset hardening and OCP WARN suppression

Add two findings to patch 1 (USB transport reliability):
- §17: suppress WARN_ON_ONCE in OCP accessors on negative error returns
- §18: guard rtl8152_post_reset against unplugged device, check enable()

Triggered by observed PCIe AER → ENODEV → cascading WARN stack traces
during USB reset recovery on device disconnect under medium load.

commit.message:
Label printing: auto-connect printer names, streamlined print flow, BLE cancel fix

- Admin printer settings: add editable list of printer device names (stored
  server-side as JSON); supports multiple names for BLE and desktop matching
- Android BLE (MakeIDPrintPlugin): when device names are configured, pre-filter
  the SDK BLE picker with setSearchDeviceStartWith so only the matching printer
  appears; falls back to unfiltered picker when no names saved
- Android BLE: add 45s timeout to openSdkDialog so cancelling the BLE picker
  (which fires no SDK callback) no longer hangs the app on "Printing…"
- Android BLE: add Cancel button in MakeIDPrinterModal during printing stage,
  calls MakeIDPrint.disconnect() and closes the modal cleanly
- Desktop (WewinPrinterModal): check admin-configured device names first before
  localStorage; auto-selects matching printer so user can print immediately
- New slab flow: remove intermediate "Print label?" confirmation modal; after
  save, go directly to BLE picker (mobile) or WeWin printer modal (desktop)
- Existing slab QR modal: remove Export Label button; Print button closes the
  QR modal before opening BLE/desktop printer UI, eliminating the back-to-modal
  cancel loop; reset mobilePrinting state on each modal open to prevent stale
  "Printing…" display on re-open
- Backend: add printer_device_names field to printer settings GET/PUT endpoints,
  stored as JSON in AppSetting key-value table
- README: update printer settings section to document auto-connect names,
  label format/margin options, and streamlined print flow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite mario-bot optimizer: frame database with adaptive convergence-based splicing

Replace the genetic algorithm with a three-phase optimizer:
1. Collection: random playthroughs with per-frame state recording
2. Splice: adaptive convergence-based splicing that backs up from
   slow spots, searches for state-compatible donors, and finds
   natural convergence points (variable length per donor)
3. Refinement: frontier exploration + targeted obstacle runs +
   noisy variants with section record tracking

Key features:
- Section record book persists fastest known inputs per level section
- Slow spot analysis identifies where golden runs lose time
- Targeted generation creates purpose-built runs for specific obstacles
- Three-part splicing: golden prefix + donor middle + golden suffix
- Hall of fame with diversity enforcement and raw input storage
- Analysis page (analysis.html) with speed profiles and bottleneck charts
- Fix bot.js replay: check level completion before death detection
  (flag sequence passes through "dead" player states)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Nim scanner running src-only checks against test files

The native Nim scanner already differentiates src/ vs tests/ via the
`inTests` flag, but three checks ignored it: hardcoded_value (let/const
literals), hardcoded_url, and top_level_var. This made tests with
expected values, mock URLs, or top-level helper state warn unnecessarily.

Add regression tests for each check.

Bumps to v0.5.14.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: USB sweep source switching — correct method name, source string, and device selection (#57)

Three bugs prevented the USB playback route from working end-to-end:

1. MinidspSweepContext called get_status() (doesn't exist on MinidspClient).
   Fix: use get_device_status() — the correct method.

2. MinidspSweepContext called switch_source("USB") but minidsp-rs daemon
   expects "Usb" (case-sensitive). Fix: use "Usb" and update VALID_SOURCES
   to match the daemon's enum (Analog, Toslink, Usb, Spdif, Aes).

3. measure() selected the output device for hdmi route but not usb route,
   so PyTTa played through the system default (ALSA "default") instead of
   the miniDSP USB device. Fix: add elif route == "usb" block that finds
   the miniDSP by playback_device name and sets sd.default.device.

4. calibrate_level() only handled HDMI/AVR ramp logic; USB mode had no path.
   Fix: add USB fast-path that just checks SNR with the miniDSP context
   and reports if the sub volume needs turning up.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix FB deduplication + fbp coverage

Root causes of 0% dedup + 2% event coverage:
1. Pixel auto-PageView fired without eventID on init
2. CAPI PageView sent before _fbp cookie was set by Pixel
3. PageView CAPI had no user data enrichment

Fixes:
- Added autoConfig=false before fbq init to prevent duplicate PageView
- CAPI PageView now delayed 1.5s so _fbp cookie is available (~100% coverage)
- PageView CAPI enriched with user_id/email/phone/name when logged in
- These changes should bring dedup from 0% to ~95%+ and event coverage
  from 2% to 75%+

crash in lichess_bot.py that killed the bot mid-game.

commit.message:
feat: improve endgame evaluation - rook vs minor scaling, passed pawn king proximity

- Rook vs minor piece endgame scaling: drawish without pawns (score/2),
  reduced with few pawns (score*3/4)
- Passed pawn king proximity bonus in queenless endgames: rewards
  king near own advanced pawns, penalizes king near enemy advanced pawns

Fixes Ne3 knight endgame blunder from debzero loss.
Blunder positions: 4/5 pass (was 2/5 before endgame eval).
